summary: Hub has 11 uncommitted changes causing drift; app faith content is thin vs website promises; need CEO scope decision.

participants.name: Theo
participants.description: Orchestrates all operations. The right hand.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good morning, team. Today's audit is about drift — are our three programs aligned across the website, the Hub, and the Lever app? From what I can see, we've got 11 uncommitted changes sitting in the Hub, the app has only 7 faith lessons which feels thin compared to what we're promising on the website, and our content pipeline needs a sync check. Let's run through it. Elon, start us off on the technical side.

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Right, so the 11 uncommitted Hub changes are the biggest red flag for ecosystem drift — every one of those is a potential mismatch between what the Hub shows and what the website references. I'll get those committed today. On the app side, the Family tab spec is at 455 lines and growing, and I need to draft the feature spec for expanding it so we're not building blind. I'll also review the family touchpoint tracker design for the 3 family members we're tracking — that needs to match what we surface on the website profile pages.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Content drift is real. The website talks about faith dimensions but the app only has 7 lessons — that's a gap people will notice. I need to draft additional faith lesson content for Lever and get the daily devotional engine running across all 8 faith dimensions so the Hub, app, and website are all speaking the same language. I'll also queue up the next blog post to tie these programs together publicly, and I've got a reminder to prep for Jett's vaccine coming up on the family side.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. So the picture is clear — Hub is ahead of what's committed, app content is behind what's promised, and the blog needs to bridge the narrative. Quick cross-talk before I close. Elon, once you commit those 11 changes, does that create any downstream issues for what Alex is drafting?

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Shouldn't — as long as Alex drafts the faith lessons against the current schema. Alex, just make sure you're pulling the 8 dimensions from the Hub source of truth, not the website copy, since the website might be slightly stale until I push.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: That's exactly right, and honestly that proves the drift point. I'll use the Hub as canonical. But Elon, we need Joshua to weigh in on whether we're expanding faith lessons to cover all 8 dimensions or keeping it focused — that changes my content volume significantly.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Agreed, that's a CEO call. Here's the close. Priority one: Elon commits the 11 Hub changes today to eliminate the primary drift source. Priority two: Alex drafts faith lesson content and daily devotionals aligned to the Hub as source of truth. Priority three: Elon specs out the Family tab expansion so we stop building ad hoc. We need Joshua to decide on the faith dimension scope for lesson content. Let's lock it in.

actionItems.text: Draft faith lesson content for the Lever app (currently 7 lessons)
Generate daily devotional content based on 8 faith dimensions
Draft feature spec for expanding Family tab (currently 455 lines)
Review family touchpoint tracker design for 3 family members
Prepare reminders for upcoming family events: Jetts vaccine

topPriorities: Commit 11 uncommitted Hub changes to eliminate primary drift source
Draft faith lesson and devotional content aligned to Hub as source of truth
Spec out Family tab expansion to prevent ad hoc feature drift

ceo_questions.question: Should faith lesson content in Lever expand to cover all 8 faith dimensions, or stay focused on a smaller subset?
ceo_questions.context: The app currently has only 7 lessons while the website references 8 faith dimensions — expanding coverage significantly increases content workload but closes the ecosystem gap.
options: Pick top 3-4 dimensions and expand incrementally
Keep current 7 lessons and refine quality first
